comment_text.sn14.31:3.1: The four noble truths were similarly extolled in the first discourse (SN 56.11), and this was subsequently said of several major teaching frameworks. Seeing one part of the Dhamma implies seeing them all.

root_text.sn14.31:1.2: “Pubbeva me, bhikkhave, sambodhā anabhisambuddhassa bodhisattasseva sato etadahosi:
root_text.sn14.31:1.3: ‘ko nu kho pathavīdhātuyā assādo, ko ādīnavo, kiṁ nissaraṇaṁ;
root_text.sn14.31:1.4: ko āpodhātuyā assādo, ko ādīnavo, kiṁ nissaraṇaṁ;
root_text.sn14.31:1.5: ko tejodhātuyā assādo, ko ādīnavo, kiṁ nissaraṇaṁ;
root_text.sn14.31:1.6: ko vāyodhātuyā assādo, ko ādīnavo, kiṁ nissaraṇan’ti?
root_text.sn14.31:2.2: ‘yaṁ kho pathavīdhātuṁ paṭicca uppajjati sukhaṁ somanassaṁ, ayaṁ pathavīdhātuyā assādo;
root_text.sn14.31:2.3: yaṁ pathavīdhātu aniccā dukkhā vipariṇāmadhammā, ayaṁ pathavīdhātuyā ādīnavo;
root_text.sn14.31:2.4: yo pathavīdhātuyā chandarāgavinayo chandarāgappahānaṁ, idaṁ pathavīdhātuyā nissaraṇaṁ.
root_text.sn14.31:2.7: yaṁ vāyodhātuṁ paṭicca uppajjati sukhaṁ somanassaṁ, ayaṁ vāyodhātuyā assādo;
root_text.sn14.31:2.8: yaṁ vāyodhātu aniccā dukkhā vipariṇāmadhammā, ayaṁ vāyodhātuyā ādīnavo;
root_text.sn14.31:2.9: yo vāyodhātuyā chandarāgavinayo chandarāgappahānaṁ, idaṁ vāyodhātuyā nissaraṇaṁ’.
root_text.sn14.31:3.1: Yāvakīvañcāhaṁ, bhikkhave, imāsaṁ catunnaṁ dhātūnaṁ evaṁ assādañca assādato ādīnavañca ādīnavato nissaraṇañca nissaraṇato yathābhūtaṁ na abbhaññāsiṁ, neva tāvāhaṁ, bhikkhave, sadevake loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya anuttaraṁ sammāsambodhiṁ abhisambuddhoti paccaññāsiṁ.
root_text.sn14.31:4.1: Yato ca khvāhaṁ, bhikkhave, imāsaṁ catunnaṁ dhātūnaṁ evaṁ assādañca assādato ādīnavañca ādīnavato nissaraṇañca nissaraṇato yathābhūtaṁ abbhaññāsiṁ, athāhaṁ, bhikkhave, sadevake loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya anuttaraṁ sammāsambodhiṁ abhisambuddhoti paccaññāsiṁ.
root_text.sn14.31:4.3: ‘akuppā me vimutti, ayamantimā jāti, natthi dāni punabbhavo’”ti.

translation_text.sn14.31:1.2: “Mendicants, before my awakening—when I was still unawakened but intent on awakening—I thought:
translation_text.sn14.31:1.3: ‘What’s the gratification, the drawback, and the escape when it comes to the earth element …
translation_text.sn14.31:2.2: ‘The pleasure and happiness that arise from the earth element: this is its gratification.
translation_text.sn14.31:2.3: That the earth element is impermanent, suffering, and perishable: this is its drawback.
translation_text.sn14.31:2.4: Removing and giving up desire and greed for the earth element: this is its escape.
translation_text.sn14.31:2.5: The pleasure and happiness that arise from the water element …
translation_text.sn14.31:2.6: The pleasure and happiness that arise from the fire element …
translation_text.sn14.31:2.7: The pleasure and happiness that arise from the air element: this is its gratification.
translation_text.sn14.31:2.8: That the air element is impermanent, suffering, and perishable: this is its drawback.
translation_text.sn14.31:2.9: Removing and giving up desire and greed for the air element: this is its escape.’
translation_text.sn14.31:3.1: As long as I didn’t truly understand these four elements’ gratification, drawback, and escape in this way for what they are, I didn’t announce my supreme perfect awakening in this world with its gods, Māras, and Divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its gods and humans.
translation_text.sn14.31:4.1: But when I did truly understand these four elements’ gratification, drawback, and escape in this way for what they are, I announced my supreme perfect awakening in this world with its gods, Māras, and Divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its gods and humans.
translation_text.sn14.31:4.2: Knowledge and vision arose in me:
translation_text.sn14.31:4.3: ‘My freedom is unshakable; this is my last rebirth; now there’ll be no more future lives.’”

reference_text.sn14.31:1.1: csp1ed12.380, csp2ed12.380, ms12S2_789, msdiv115, ndp13.144, pts-vp-pli2.170
